channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft: Hackers abuse OAuth error flows to spread malware
item.description: Hackers are abusing the legitimate OAuth redirection mechanism to bypass phishing protections in email and browsers to take users to malicious pages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Vehicle Tire Pressure Sensors Enable Silent Tracking
item.description: Like many other features and systems in modern cars, tire pressure sensors leak sensitive data that can be abused by threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
item.description: The exploitation activity against CVE-2026-21385, a high-severity memory corruption flaw, could be tied to commercial spyware or nation-state threat groups....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Speakeasies to Shadow AI: Banning AI Browsers Will Fail
item.description: Lessons from history highlight why AI-enabled browsers require controlled enablement....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data exfiltr...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Chrome shifts to two-week release cycle for increased stability
item.description: Google Chrome will shift from a four-week to a two-week release cycle to roll out new features, bug fixes, and performance improvements more frequently. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LexisNexis confirms data breach as hackers leak stolen files
item.description: American data analytics company LexisNexis Legal & Professional has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached its servers and accessed some customer and business information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Compromised Site Management Panels are a Hot Item in Cybercrime Markets
item.description: Compromised cPanel credentials are being sold in bulk across underground channels as plug-and-play phishing and scam infrastructure. Flare explains how analyzing 200,000 underground posts reveals a co...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Building a High-Impact Tier 1: The 3 Steps CISOs Must Follow
item.description: Every CISO knows the uncomfortable truth about their Security Operations Center: the people most responsible for catching threats in real time are the people with the least experience. Tier 1 analysts...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Open-Source CyberStrikeAI Deployed in AI-Driven FortiGate Attacks Across 55 Countries
item.description: The threat actor behind the recently disclosed artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate appliances leveraged an open-source, AI-native security testing platform call...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Agent Overload: How to Solve the Workload Identity Crisis
item.description: Workloads keep getting more complicated and organizations are struggling to keep up. So what's the play?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Amazon: Drone strikes damaged AWS data centers in Middle East
item.description: Amazon has confirmed that three Amazon Web Services (AWS) data centers in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) and one in Bahrain have been damaged by drone strikes, causing an extensive outage that is stil...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: As War Continues, Pro-Iranian Actors Launch Barrage of Cyberattacks
item.description: Iran and its supporters have taken to cyberspace to retaliate for US-Israeli military action, with an aim to cause economic and physical disruption....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications, APIs, a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's advertised ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Star Citizen game dev discloses breach affecting user data
item.description: Cloud Imperium Games (CIG), the game developer behind Star Citizen and Squadron 42, says attackers breached systems containing some users' personal information in January. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: UH Cancer Center data breach affects nearly 1.2 million people
item.description: The University of Hawaii confirmed that a ransomware gang stole the data of nearly 1.2 million individuals in August 2025 after breaching its Cancer Center's Epidemiology Division. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Android gets patches for Qualcomm zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: Google has released security updates to patch 129 Android security vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in a Qualcomm display component. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The act...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
item.description: Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet For...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 30 Alleged Members of 'The Com' Arrested in Project Compass
item.description: The global law enforcement crackdown, which began in January 2025, also identified nearly 180 members of the notorious cybercriminal collective....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Google Security site uses PWA app to steal credentials, MFA codes
item.description: A phishing campaign is using a fake Google Account security page to deliver a web-based app capable of stealing one-time passcodes, harvesting cryptocurrency wallet addresses, and proxying attacker tr...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Alabama man pleads guilty to hacking, extorting hundreds of women
item.description: A 22-year-old Alabama man pleaded guilty to extortion, cyberstalking, and computer fraud charges after hijacking the social media accounts of hundreds of young women (including minors). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Florida woman imprisoned for massive Microsoft license fraud scheme
item.description: A Florida woman was sentenced to 22 months in prison for running a massive years-long scheme to traffic thousands of stolen Microsoft Certificate of Authenticity (COA) labels. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Develops Merkle Tree Certificates to Enable Quantum-Resistant HTTPS in Chrome
item.description: Google has announced a new program in its Chrome browser to ensure that HTTPS certificates are secure against the future risk posed by quantum computers. "To ensure the scalability and efficiency of t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
